  • Spread a thin layer of enchilada sauce on the bottom of the baking dish.
  • Warm the corn tortillas briefly so they are pliable, then fill each with the cannabis-infused chicken mixture and a sprinkle of cheese. Roll tightly and place seam-side down in the dish.
  • Pour the remaining enchilada sauce over the rolled tortillas and top with the rest of the shredded cheese.
  • Bake for 20-25 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbly. Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.

recipes.name: Cannabis Tamales
recipes.description: Traditional cannabis-infused tamales made with masa dough and a savory pork filling, steamed in corn husks for an authentic Mexican experience with a gentle THC kick.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 2 cups masa harina
  • 1 cup chicken broth (warm)
  • 1/2 cup lard or vegetable shortening
  • 1 1/2 cups shredded cooked pork
  • 1 cup red chili sauce
  • 1 teaspoon baking powder
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 20 dried corn husks, soaked in warm water
recipes.instructions:
• Soak the dried corn husks in warm water for at least 30 minutes until pliable. Combine the shredded pork with red chili sauce and set aside.
  • Beat the lard or shortening with the softened cannabutter until fluffy. Gradually mix in the masa harina, baking powder, salt, and warm broth until a smooth, spreadable dough forms.
  • Spread about 2 tablespoons of the cannabis-infused masa dough onto each corn husk, leaving a border on the edges. Place a spoonful of the pork filling in the center.
  • Fold the sides of the husk over the filling, then fold the bottom up. Tie with a thin strip of corn husk if needed.
  • Arrange the tamales upright in a steamer basket, cover, and steam for 60-75 minutes until the dough pulls away from the husk cleanly.
  • Let rest for 10 minutes before unwrapping and serving with extra chili sauce on the side.

recipes.name: Cannabis Chilaquiles
recipes.description: Hearty cannabis-infused chilaquiles featuring crispy tortilla chips simmered in tangy salsa verde, topped with crema and a fried egg for a classic Mexican breakfast.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 6 corn tortillas, cut into triangles and fried (or tortilla chips)
  • 2 cups salsa verde
  • 1/2 cup crumbled queso fresco
  • 1/4 cup Mexican crema or sour cream
  • 4 large eggs
  • 1/4 cup diced white onion
  • Fresh cilantro for garnish
  • 1 avocado, sliced
recipes.instructions:
• Melt the cannabutter in a large skillet over medium-low heat. Add the salsa verde and stir to combine. Let it warm through for 2-3 minutes.
  • Add the fried tortilla chips to the skillet and gently toss to coat them evenly in the cannabis-infused salsa. Cook for 2-3 minutes until the chips soften slightly but still have some texture.
  • In a separate pan, fry the eggs sunny-side up to your liking.
  • Divide the chilaquiles among plates, top each with a fried egg, crumbled queso fresco, a drizzle of crema, diced onion, sliced avocado, and fresh cilantro.

recipes.name: Cannabis Elote
recipes.description: Cannabis-infused Mexican street corn slathered with creamy cannabutter, cotija cheese, chili powder, and lime for an irresistible THC-infused side dish.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 6 ears of fresh corn, husked
  • 1/3 cup mayonnaise
  • 1/2 cup crumbled cotija cheese
  • 1 teaspoon chili powder (or Tajín)
  • 2 limes, cut into wedges
  • 2 tablespoons fresh cilantro, chopped
  • Pinch of salt
recipes.instructions:
• Grill or boil the corn ears until tender and lightly charred, about 8-10 minutes on the grill or 6-8 minutes in boiling water.
  • Melt the cannabutter gently in a small saucepan over low heat. Mix together the melted cannabutter and mayonnaise until smooth.
  • Brush each ear of corn generously with the cannabis-infused mayo-butter mixture while the corn is still warm.
  • Roll or sprinkle each ear with crumbled cotija cheese, then dust with chili powder. Squeeze lime juice over the top and garnish with chopped cilantro.
  • Serve immediately while warm. Each ear represents one portion.

recipes.name: Cannabis Churro Bites
recipes.description: Golden, cinnamon-sugar cannabis churro bites made with cannabutter-enriched dough, fried to perfection and served with a warm chocolate dipping sauce.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1 cup water
  • 2 tablespoons regular butter
  • 2 tablespoons granulated sugar
  • 1/4 teaspoon salt
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 2 large eggs
  • 1/2 cup sugar mixed with 1 tablespoon ground cinnamon (for coating)
  • Vegetable oil for frying
recipes.instructions:
• In a medium saucepan, combine water, regular butter, cannabutter, sugar, and salt. Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring until the butters are fully melted.
  • Remove from heat and stir in the flour all at once, mixing vigorously until a smooth dough ball forms and pulls away from the sides of the pan.
  • Let the dough cool for 2-3 minutes, then beat in the eggs one at a time until the dough is smooth and glossy. Transfer to a piping bag fitted with a star tip.
  • Heat vegetable oil to 350°F (175°C) in a deep pot. Pipe 1-2 inch lengths of dough directly into the oil, cutting with scissors. Fry in batches for 2-3 minutes until golden brown.
  • Remove with a slotted spoon, drain briefly on paper towels, then immediately toss in the cinnamon-sugar mixture.
  • Serve warm with chocolate sauce for dipping.

recipes.name: Cannabis Pozole
recipes.description: A warming cannabis-infused pozole rojo with tender pork, hominy, and a deeply flavored dried chili broth — a traditional Mexican comfort soup with a medicated twist.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabis-infused coconut oil
  • 1 1/2 lbs pork shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
  • 1 can (29 oz) white hominy, drained and rinsed
  • 4 dried guajillo chiles, stemmed and seeded
  • 2 dried ancho chiles, stemmed and seeded
  • 6 cups chicken broth
  • 1 white onion, quartered
  • 4 cloves garlic
  • 1 teaspoon dried oregano (Mexican oregano preferred)
  • Toppings: shredded cabbage, sliced radishes, lime wedges, tostadas
recipes.instructions:
• Toast the dried guajillo and ancho chiles in a dry skillet for 1-2 minutes per side until fragrant. Soak in hot water for 15 minutes, then blend with 1 cup of the soaking liquid, garlic, and half the onion until smooth. Strain through a sieve.
  • Heat the cannabis-infused coconut oil in a large pot over medium heat. Sear the pork cubes in batches until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per batch.
  • Add the chile sauce to the pot and cook for 3-4 minutes, stirring frequently. Pour in the chicken broth, add the remaining onion and oregano, and bring to a boil.
  • Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 1 to 1.5 hours until the pork is fork-tender.
  • Add the drained hominy and cook for an additional 15 minutes. Season with salt to taste.
  • Serve in deep bowls with shredded cabbage, sliced radishes, lime wedges, and tostadas on the side.

recipes.name: Cannabis Mole Chicken
recipes.description: Cannabis-infused chicken mole featuring a complex sauce of dried chiles, chocolate, and spices — a rich, deeply flavored Mexican classic with controlled THC dosing.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 4 bone-in chicken thighs
  • 3 dried ancho chiles, stemmed and seeded
  • 2 dried pasilla chiles, stemmed and seeded
  • 1 oz Mexican chocolate (such as Ibarra), chopped
  • 1/4 cup sesame seeds
  • 1/2 cup diced white onion
  • 2 cloves garlic
  • 1 cup chicken broth
  • Salt and toasted sesame seeds for garnish
recipes.instructions:
• Toast the dried chiles in a dry skillet until fragrant, about 1-2 minutes per side. Soak in hot water for 15 minutes, then drain.
  • In the same skillet, toast the sesame seeds until golden. Blend the rehydrated chiles, sesame seeds, onion, garlic, and chicken broth until smooth.
  • Melt the cannabutter in a large deep skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat. Season the chicken thighs with salt and sear skin-side down for 4-5 minutes until golden. Flip and sear another 2 minutes. Remove and set aside.
  • Pour the mole sauce into the same pan and cook for 5 minutes, stirring often. Add the chopped chocolate and stir until melted and incorporated.
  • Return the chicken to the pan, spoon sauce over the top, cover, and simmer on low for 30-35 minutes until the chicken is cooked through (165°F internal).
  • Serve over rice, garnished with toasted sesame seeds and accompanied by warm tortillas.

recipes.name: Cannabis Tostadas
recipes.description: Crispy cannabis-infused tostadas loaded with seasoned refried beans, fresh toppings, and a drizzle of cannabutter crema for a quick and flavorful Mexican meal.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 6 crispy tostada shells
  • 1 can (15 oz) refried beans
  • 1 cup shredded lettuce
  • 1 cup diced tomatoes
  • 1/2 cup crumbled cotija cheese
  • 1/4 cup Mexican crema or sour cream
  • 1 avocado, sliced
  • Hot sauce to taste
recipes.instructions:
• Melt the cannabutter in a small saucepan over low heat. Stir the melted cannabutter into the Mexican crema until evenly combined. Set aside.
  • Warm the refried beans in a saucepan over medium heat, stirring until heated through and spreadable.
  • Spread a generous layer of warm refried beans on each tostada shell.
  • Top with shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, sliced avocado, and crumbled cotija cheese.
  • Drizzle each tostada with the cannabis-infused crema and a dash of hot sauce. Serve immediately.

recipes.name: Cannabis Birria Tacos
recipes.description: Tender cannabis-infused birria beef tucked into crispy dipped tortillas with melted cheese, served alongside a rich consommé for dunking — the ultimate THC taco experience.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabis-infused cooking oil
  • 2 lbs beef chuck roast, cut into large chunks
  • 4 dried guajillo chiles, stemmed and seeded
  • 2 dried ancho chiles, stemmed and seeded
  • 1 can (14 oz) diced fire-roasted tomatoes
  • 1 white onion, quartered
  • 4 cloves garlic
  • 1 tablespoon apple cider vinegar
  • 1 teaspoon each: cumin, oregano, black pepper
  • 12 corn tortillas and 1 1/2 cups shredded Oaxaca or mozzarella cheese
recipes.instructions:
• Toast the dried chiles in a dry skillet for 1-2 minutes per side. Soak in hot water for 15 minutes, then blend with the tomatoes, half the onion, garlic, vinegar, and spices until smooth.
  • Heat the cannabis-infused cooking oil in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the beef chunks on all sides until well browned, about 6-8 minutes total.
  • Pour the blended chile sauce over the beef, add the remaining onion, and enough water to just cover the meat. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a low simmer, cover, and braise for 2.5 to 3 hours until the beef shreds easily.
  • Remove the beef, shred with two forks, and return to the broth. Season the consommé with salt to taste.
  • Dip corn tortillas into the consommé and cook on a hot griddle. Add shredded cheese and birria meat to one side, fold over, and cook until crispy on both sides and the cheese is melted.
  • Serve the tacos alongside small bowls of hot consommé garnished with diced onion, cilantro, and lime wedges for dipping.

recipes.name: Cannabis Tres Leches Cake
recipes.description: A luscious cannabis-infused tres leches cake soaked in three milks, topped with whipped cream — this iconic Mexican dessert delivers a perfectly dosed, melt-in-your-mouth treat.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 5 large eggs, separated
  • 1 cup granulated sugar
  • 1/3 cup whole milk
  • 1 can (14 oz) sweetened condensed milk
  • 1 can (12 oz) evaporated milk
  • 1 cup heavy whipping cream (for soaking) + 2 cups (for topping)
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • Ground cinnamon for garnish
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a 9x13 baking pan. Melt the cannabutter and let it cool to room temperature.
  • Beat the egg yolks with 3/4 cup sugar until pale and thick. Stir in the melted cannabutter, whole milk, and vanilla. Fold in the flour gently.
  • In a separate bowl, whip the egg whites with the remaining 1/4 cup sugar until stiff peaks form. Fold into the batter in thirds. Pour into the prepared pan.
  • Bake for 25-30 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean. Let cool completely in the pan.
  • Whisk together the condensed milk, evaporated milk, and 1 cup heavy cream. Poke holes all over the cooled cake with a fork, then slowly pour the three-milk mixture over the cake. Refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.
  • Whip the remaining 2 cups heavy cream with 2 tablespoons sugar until stiff. Spread over the soaked cake, dust with cinnamon, and serve chilled.

recipes.name: Cannabis Red Velvet Cake
recipes.description: A stunning cannabis-infused red velvet cake with a tender crumb and tangy cream cheese frosting, perfectly dosed for a decadent dessert experience.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 2 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 1/2 cups granulated sugar
  • 1/2 cup regular butter, softened
  • 2 large eggs
  • 1 cup buttermilk
  • 2 tablespoons red food coloring
  • 2 tablespoons unsweetened cocoa powder
  • 1 teaspoon each: vanilla extract, white vinegar, baking soda
  • 8 oz cream cheese, 1/2 cup butter, 3 cups powdered sugar (for frosting)
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour two 9-inch round cake pans. Melt the cannabutter and let it cool slightly.
  • Cream together the softened regular butter, melted cannabutter, and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then add the vanilla.
  • Mix the cocoa powder with the red food coloring to form a paste and add to the butter mixture. Alternate adding the flour and buttermilk in three additions, mixing until just combined.
  • Stir together the vinegar and baking soda, then gently fold into the batter. Divide between the prepared pans.
  • Bake for 28-32 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean. Cool in pans for 10 minutes, then turn out onto wire racks to cool completely.
  • For the frosting, beat the cream cheese and butter until smooth, then gradually add powdered sugar and vanilla. Frost between the layers and over the entire cake. Refrigerate until serving.

recipes.name: Cannabis Snickerdoodle Cookies
recipes.description: Soft and chewy cannabis-infused snickerdoodle cookies rolled in cinnamon sugar, delivering classic comfort-cookie flavor with a precisely dosed THC boost.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1/2 cup regular butter, softened
  • 1 1/2 cups granulated sugar
  • 2 large eggs
  • 2 3/4 cups all-purpose flour
  • 2 teaspoons cream of tartar
  • 1 teaspoon baking soda
  • 1/4 teaspoon salt
  • 3 tablespoons sugar mixed with 2 teaspoons cinnamon (for rolling)
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
  • Cream together the softened regular butter, softened cannabutter, and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the eggs one at a time.
  • In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, cream of tartar, baking soda, and salt. Gradually mix the dry ingredients into the butter mixture until a soft dough forms.
  • Roll the dough into 1-inch balls and coat each one in the cinnamon-sugar mixture. Place on the prepared baking sheets 2 inches apart.
  • Bake for 10-12 minutes until the edges are set but the centers are still slightly soft. The cookies will firm up as they cool.
  • Let cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack. Makes approximately 24 cookies.

recipes.name: Cannabis Pumpkin Pie
recipes.description: A warmly spiced cannabis-infused pumpkin pie with a flaky crust and velvety filling, perfect for holiday gatherings or any time you want an infused seasonal treat.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1 unbaked 9-inch pie crust
  • 1 can (15 oz) pure pumpkin puree
  • 1 can (14 oz) sweetened condensed milk
  • 2 large eggs
  • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
  • 1/2 teaspoon each: ground ginger, ground nutmeg
  • 1/4 teaspoon ground cloves
  • 1/4 teaspoon salt
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Place the pie crust in a 9-inch pie plate and crimp the edges.
  • Melt the cannabutter gently over low heat and let it cool slightly.
  • In a large bowl, whisk together the pumpkin puree, sweetened condensed milk, eggs, melted cannabutter, and all the spices until smooth and well combined.
  • Pour the cannabis-infused filling into the pie crust.
  • Bake at 425°F for 15 minutes, then reduce the temperature to 350°F (175°C) and continue baking for 35-40 minutes until the filling is set and a knife inserted near the center comes out clean.
  • Cool completely on a wire rack, then refrigerate for at least 2 hours before slicing. Serve with whipped cream.

recipes.name: Cannabis Strawberry Cheesecake Bars
recipes.description: Creamy cannabis-infused strawberry cheesecake bars with a buttery graham cracker crust and a fresh strawberry swirl — a perfectly portioned THC dessert.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1 1/2 cups graham cracker crumbs
  • 1/4 cup regular butter, melted
  • 16 oz cream cheese, softened
  • 1/2 cup granulated sugar
  • 2 large eggs
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 1/2 cup fresh strawberries, pureed
  • 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C). Line an 8x8 baking pan with parchment paper, leaving overhang for easy removal.
  • Melt the cannabutter and regular butter together over low heat. Mix with the graham cracker crumbs and press firmly into the bottom of the prepared pan. Bake for 8 minutes, then let cool.
  • Beat the cream cheese and sugar until smooth. Add the eggs one at a time, then mix in the vanilla and flour until just combined. Do not overmix.
  • Pour the cheesecake filling over the crust. Drop spoonfuls of strawberry puree on top and use a knife to create a swirl pattern.
  • Bake for 35-40 minutes until the edges are set but the center has a slight jiggle. Turn off the oven, crack the door, and let the bars cool inside for 30 minutes.
  • Refrigerate for at least 4 hours before slicing into bars. Cut into 9 equal portions for consistent dosing.

recipes.name: Cannabis Oatmeal Raisin Cookies
recipes.description: Chewy cannabis-infused oatmeal raisin cookies packed with oats, plump raisins, and warm spices for a wholesome, medicated twist on a timeless cookie classic.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1/2 cup regular butter, softened
  • 3/4 cup packed brown sugar
  • 1/4 cup granulated sugar
  • 1 large egg
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1 1/2 cups old-fashioned rolled oats
  • 1/2 teaspoon each: baking soda, cinnamon, salt
  • 3/4 cup raisins
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
  • Cream together the softened regular butter, softened cannabutter, brown sugar, and granulated sugar until fluffy. Beat in the egg and vanilla extract.
  • Whisk together the flour, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt. Stir the dry ingredients into the butter mixture until just combined. Fold in the oats and raisins.
  • Drop rounded tablespoons of dough onto the prepared baking sheets, spacing them 2 inches apart.
  • Bake for 10-13 minutes until the edges are golden but the centers are still slightly soft.
  • Cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack. Makes approximately 24 cookies.

recipes.name: Cannabis Key Lime Pie
recipes.description: A tangy and refreshing cannabis-infused key lime pie with a buttery graham cracker crust and a silky citrus filling, topped with fluffy whipped cream.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1 1/2 cups graham cracker crumbs
  • 1/3 cup regular butter, melted
  • 3 egg yolks
  • 1 can (14 oz) sweetened condensed milk
  • 2/3 cup fresh key lime juice (about 20 key limes)
  • 1 tablespoon key lime zest
  • 1 1/2 cups heavy whipping cream
  • 2 tablespoons powdered sugar
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Melt the cannabutter and regular butter together over low heat. Mix with the graham cracker crumbs and press firmly into the bottom and sides of a 9-inch pie plate. Bake for 8 minutes, then let cool.
  • Whisk the egg yolks until pale. Add the sweetened condensed milk, key lime juice, and lime zest. Mix until smooth and slightly thickened.
  • Pour the filling into the cooled cannabis-infused crust.
  • Bake for 15 minutes until the filling is just set. It should still have a slight wobble in the center. Let cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 4 hours.
  • Before serving, whip the heavy cream with powdered sugar until stiff peaks form. Spread or pipe over the chilled pie. Garnish with lime zest or thin lime slices.

recipes.name: Cannabis French Toast
recipes.description: Thick-cut cannabis-infused French toast soaked in a rich custard and pan-fried in cannabutter until golden, topped with maple syrup and fresh berries.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 6 slices thick-cut brioche or challah bread
  • 3 large eggs
  • 3/4 cup whole milk
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
  • 2 tablespoons regular butter
  • Maple syrup and fresh berries for serving
  • Powdered sugar for dusting
recipes.instructions:
• Whisk together the eggs, milk, vanilla extract, and cinnamon in a shallow dish until well combined.
  • Melt the cannabutter and regular butter together in a large skillet or griddle over medium-low heat. Keep the heat gentle to preserve the cannabutter's potency.
  • Dip each slice of bread into the egg mixture, letting it soak for about 15 seconds per side.
  • Cook the soaked bread in the cannabis-infused butter for 3-4 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through.
  • Serve immediately, topped with maple syrup, fresh berries, and a dusting of powdered sugar.

recipes.name: Cannabis Crème Brûlée
recipes.description: Silky cannabis-infused crème brûlée with a crisp caramelized sugar top, delivering an elegant and precisely dosed THC dessert experience.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 2 cups heavy cream
  • 5 large egg yolks
  • 1/2 cup granulated sugar, plus extra for topping
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • Pinch of salt
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C). Place 6 ramekins in a deep baking dish.
  • In a small saucepan, warm the heavy cream and cannabutter over medium-low heat until the cannabutter is fully melted and the cream just begins to steam. Do not boil. Remove from heat and stir in the vanilla.
  • In a bowl, whisk the egg yolks, sugar, and salt until smooth and pale. Slowly pour the warm cannabis-infused cream into the egg mixture, whisking constantly to temper the eggs.
  • Strain the custard through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps. Divide evenly among the ramekins.
  • Pour hot water into the baking dish until it reaches halfway up the sides of the ramekins. Bake for 40-45 minutes until the custards are set around the edges but still slightly jiggly in the center.
  • Refrigerate for at least 4 hours. Before serving, sprinkle a thin layer of sugar on each custard and caramelize with a kitchen torch or under the broiler until golden and crackly.

recipes.name: Cannabis S'mores Bars
recipes.description: Gooey cannabis-infused s'mores bars layered with a graham cracker base, melted chocolate, and toasted marshmallows — campfire flavor with a medicated twist.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1/4 cup regular butter, melted
  • 2 cups graham cracker crumbs
  • 1/4 cup packed brown sugar
  • 2 cups semi-sweet chocolate chips
  • 3 cups mini marshmallows
  • 1/4 cup heavy cream
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line a 9x9 baking pan with parchment paper.
  • Melt the cannabutter and regular butter together over low heat. Mix with the graham cracker crumbs and brown sugar. Press firmly into the bottom of the prepared pan.
  • Bake the crust for 8 minutes. Remove from oven and set the oven to broil.
  • Heat the chocolate chips and heavy cream in a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second intervals, stirring between each, until smooth. Pour the chocolate evenly over the warm crust.
  • Spread the mini marshmallows in an even layer over the chocolate. Broil for 1-2 minutes, watching constantly, until the marshmallows are golden and toasted.
  • Let cool completely before cutting into 9 bars for consistent dosing. Store at room temperature.

recipes.name: Cannabis Chocolate Chip Muffins
recipes.description: Fluffy cannabis-infused chocolate chip muffins with a tender crumb and pockets of melted chocolate — a perfectly portioned THC breakfast or snack treat.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1/4 cup regular butter, melted
  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 3/4 cup granulated sugar
  • 2 teaspoons baking powder
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 2 large eggs
  • 3/4 cup whole milk
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 1 cup semi-sweet chocolate chips
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a 12-cup muffin tin with paper liners.
  • Melt the cannabutter and regular butter together over low heat. Let cool slightly.
  • In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt. In a separate bowl, whisk the eggs, milk, vanilla, and melted cannabis-infused butter mixture.
  • Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined — do not overmix. Fold in the chocolate chips.
  • Divide the batter evenly among the 12 muffin cups, filling each about 2/3 full.
  • Bake for 18-22 minutes until the tops are golden and a toothpick comes out clean. Cool in the tin for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack.

recipes.name: Cannabis Bread Pudding
recipes.description: Rich cannabis-infused bread pudding made with cubed brioche soaked in a spiced custard and baked until golden, served warm with a bourbon vanilla sauce.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 6 cups cubed day-old brioche or French bread
  • 2 cups whole milk
  • 1 cup heavy cream
  • 4 large eggs
  • 3/4 cup granulated sugar
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
  • 1/4 teaspoon ground nutmeg
  • 1/2 cup raisins (optional)
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 9x13 baking dish with regular butter.
  • Melt the cannabutter over low heat. In a large bowl, whisk together the milk, cream, eggs, sugar, melted cannabutter, vanilla, cinnamon, and nutmeg until well combined.
  • Spread the bread cubes in the prepared baking dish and scatter the raisins over top if using. Pour the cannabis-infused custard mixture evenly over the bread. Press the bread down gently to help it absorb the liquid.
  • Let the pudding sit for 15-20 minutes so the bread fully absorbs the custard.
  • Bake for 45-50 minutes until the top is golden brown and the custard is set. A knife inserted in the center should come out clean.
  • Let cool for 10 minutes. Serve warm, drizzled with a vanilla or bourbon sauce and a scoop of vanilla ice cream.

recipes.name: Cannabis Pecan Pie
recipes.description: A decadent cannabis-infused pecan pie with a gooey caramel filling loaded with toasted pecans in a flaky crust — a Southern classic with a THC infusion.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1 unbaked 9-inch pie crust
  • 1 cup light corn syrup
  • 1 cup packed brown sugar
  • 3 large eggs
  • 2 tablespoons regular butter, melted
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 1/4 teaspoon salt
  • 1 1/2 cups pecan halves
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Place the pie crust in a 9-inch pie plate and crimp the edges decoratively.
  • Melt the cannabutter and regular butter together over low heat. Let cool slightly.
  • In a large bowl, whisk together the corn syrup, brown sugar, eggs, melted cannabis-infused butter mixture, vanilla, and salt until smooth.
  • Arrange the pecan halves in the bottom of the pie crust. Carefully pour the filling over the pecans — they will float to the top.
  • Bake for 50-60 minutes until the filling is set and the center has only a slight jiggle. If the crust browns too quickly, cover the edges with foil.
  • Cool completely on a wire rack for at least 2 hours before slicing. Serve at room temperature or slightly warm with vanilla ice cream or whipped cream.

recipes.name: Cannabis Banana Pudding
recipes.description: Creamy layered cannabis-infused banana pudding with vanilla wafers, sliced bananas, and a luscious homemade custard — a Southern favorite with gentle THC dosing.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 3/4 cup granulated sugar
  • 1/3 cup all-purpose flour
  • Pinch of salt
  • 3 large eggs, separated
  • 2 cups whole milk
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 1 box (11 oz) vanilla wafers
  • 4-5 ripe bananas, sliced
recipes.instructions:
• In a medium saucepan, whisk together 1/2 cup sugar, flour, and salt. In a separate bowl, whisk the egg yolks with the milk.
  • Gradually stir the milk mixture into the dry ingredients. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the custard thickens and begins to bubble, about 8-10 minutes.
  • Remove from heat and stir in the cannabutter and vanilla until the cannabutter is completely melted and incorporated. Let the cannabis-infused custard cool slightly.
  • In a trifle dish or 9x13 pan, layer vanilla wafers, sliced bananas, and warm custard. Repeat the layers 2-3 times, ending with custard on top.
  • Beat the egg whites with the remaining 1/4 cup sugar until stiff peaks form. Spread the meringue over the pudding and broil for 2-3 minutes until golden, or skip the meringue and top with whipped cream instead.
  • Refrigerate for at least 4 hours before serving to let the flavors meld and the wafers soften.

recipes.name: Cannabis Peanut Butter Cups
recipes.description: Homemade cannabis-infused peanut butter cups with a smooth chocolate shell and a sweetened peanut butter center — easy to make and perfectly portioned for consistent dosing.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabis-infused coconut oil
  • 2 cups semi-sweet chocolate chips
  • 1 cup creamy peanut butter
  • 1/2 cup powdered sugar
  • 1/4 teaspoon salt
  • 1 tablespoon regular butter, melted
recipes.instructions:
• Line a 12-cup muffin tin with paper liners (or use a mini muffin tin for smaller cups).
  • Melt the chocolate chips with the cannabis-infused coconut oil in a double boiler or microwave (in 30-second intervals, stirring between each) until completely smooth.
  • Spoon about 1 tablespoon of the cannabis-infused chocolate into each muffin liner and use the back of the spoon to push chocolate slightly up the sides. Freeze for 10 minutes until firm.
  • Mix together the peanut butter, powdered sugar, melted butter, and salt until smooth. Roll into 12 small discs and place one on top of each chocolate layer.
  • Pour the remaining chocolate over the peanut butter filling, covering it completely. Tap the pan on the counter to level the chocolate.
  • Refrigerate for at least 1 hour until fully set. Sprinkle with flaky sea salt before serving if desired. Store in the refrigerator.

recipes.name: Cannabis Macarons
recipes.description: Delicate cannabis-infused French macarons with crisp shells, chewy centers, and a cannabutter ganache filling — an elegant THC treat for the adventurous baker.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter (for filling)
  • 1 cup almond flour, sifted
  • 1 3/4 cups powdered sugar, sifted
  • 3 large egg whites, room temperature
  • 1/4 cup granulated sugar
  • 1/4 teaspoon cream of tartar
  • 1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • Gel food coloring (optional)
  • 4 oz white chocolate, chopped (for filling)
  • 2 tablespoons heavy cream (for filling)
recipes.instructions:
• Sift the almond flour and powdered sugar together twice into a large bowl. Set aside.
  • Beat the egg whites with cream of tartar on medium speed until foamy. Gradually add the granulated sugar and beat on high until stiff, glossy peaks form. Add vanilla and food coloring if desired.
  • Fold the almond flour mixture into the meringue in thirds using a spatula. The batter should flow like lava and form a ribbon that dissolves back into itself within 10 seconds (macaronage).
  • Pipe 1.5-inch rounds onto parchment-lined baking sheets. Tap the sheets firmly on the counter to release air bubbles. Let rest at room temperature for 30-60 minutes until the tops are dry to the touch.
  • Preheat oven to 300°F (150°C). Bake for 16-18 minutes until the shells are set and have developed ruffled "feet" at the base. Let cool completely on the baking sheet.
  • For the filling, heat the heavy cream until simmering, pour over the chopped white chocolate, and stir until smooth. Mix in the softened cannabutter until fully incorporated. Chill until spreadable. Pipe or spread filling between two matched shells. Refrigerate macarons for 24 hours before serving for the best texture. Makes about 24 shells (12 filled macarons).

recipes.name: Cannabis Mac and Cheese
recipes.description: Creamy, comforting cannabis-infused mac and cheese made with a rich cheddar sauce and cannabutter for a perfectly dosed edible lunch.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 8 oz elbow macaroni
  • 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
  • 2 cups whole milk
  • 2 cups sharp cheddar cheese, shredded
  • 1/2 cup gruyère cheese, shredded
  • 1/2 teaspoon mustard powder
  • Salt and pepper to taste
  • 1/4 cup panko breadcrumbs
recipes.instructions:
• Cook macaroni according to package directions, drain and set aside.
  • In a saucepan over medium heat, melt the cannabutter. Whisk in flour and cook for 1 minute to form a roux.
  • Gradually pour in milk, whisking constantly until the sauce thickens, about 4-5 minutes.
  • Remove from heat and stir in cheddar, gruyère, mustard powder, salt, and pepper until smooth.
  • Fold the cooked macaroni into the cheese sauce and transfer to a greased baking dish.
  • Top with panko breadcrumbs and bake at 375°F for 15-20 minutes until golden and bubbling.
  • Let cool for 5 minutes before serving. Divide into equal portions for consistent dosing.

recipes.name: Cannabis BBQ Pulled Chicken
recipes.description: Tender cannabis-infused BBQ pulled chicken slow-cooked with smoky sauce and cannabutter, perfect for sandwiches or served over rice.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1.5 lbs boneless skinless chicken breasts
  • 1 cup BBQ sauce
  • 1/4 cup chicken broth
  • 2 tablespoons apple cider vinegar
  • 1 tablespoon brown sugar
  • 1 teaspoon smoked paprika
  • 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder
  • 6 brioche buns
  • Coleslaw for topping (optional)
recipes.instructions:
• Place chicken breasts in a slow cooker. Mix BBQ sauce, chicken broth, apple cider vinegar, brown sugar, smoked paprika, and garlic powder, then pour over the chicken.
  • Cook on low for 6-7 hours or high for 3-4 hours until chicken shreds easily.
  • Remove chicken and shred with two forks. Return shredded chicken to the slow cooker.
  • Add the cannabutter to the warm pulled chicken and stir until fully melted and incorporated.
  • Let the mixture sit on warm for 10 minutes to allow even distribution.
  • Serve on brioche buns with coleslaw if desired. Divide evenly for consistent dosing.

recipes.name: Cannabis Garlic Butter Shrimp
recipes.description: Succulent cannabis-infused garlic butter shrimp sautéed with lemon and herbs, delivering a flavorful and precisely dosed edible experience.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined
  • 4 cloves garlic, minced
  • 2 tablespoons regular butter
  • Juice of 1 lemon
  • 1/4 cup dry white wine
  • 2 tablespoons fresh parsley, chopped
  • 1/2 teaspoon red pepper flakes
  • Salt and pepper to taste
recipes.instructions:
• Pat shrimp dry with paper towels and season with salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes.
  • Melt regular butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add shrimp and cook for 2 minutes per side until pink. Remove and set aside.
  • Reduce heat to medium-low. Add cannabutter and minced garlic to the skillet, stirring for 1 minute until fragrant. Do not let the garlic burn.
  • Pour in white wine and lemon juice, scraping up any browned bits. Simmer for 2 minutes.
  • Return shrimp to the skillet and toss to coat in the cannabis garlic butter sauce.
  • Garnish with fresh parsley and serve immediately over rice or with crusty bread.

recipes.name: Cannabis Chicken Alfredo
recipes.description: Rich and creamy cannabis-infused chicken Alfredo with a velvety Parmesan sauce made with cannabutter for a luxurious edible pasta dish.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 12 oz fettuccine pasta
  • 2 boneless skinless chicken breasts
  • 1 cup heavy cream
  • 1 cup freshly grated Parmesan cheese
  • 3 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 tablespoon olive oil
  • Salt and pepper to taste
  • Fresh parsley for garnish
recipes.instructions:
• Cook fettuccine according to package directions. Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water before draining.
  • Season chicken breasts with salt and pepper. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and cook chicken for 6-7 minutes per side until golden and cooked through. Slice into strips.
  • In the same skillet, reduce heat to medium-low. Add cannabutter and garlic, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
  • Pour in heavy cream and bring to a gentle simmer. Stir in Parmesan cheese until melted and smooth.
  • Add the drained fettuccine and sliced chicken to the sauce. Toss to coat, adding reserved pasta water if needed to thin the sauce.
  • Serve immediately, garnished with parsley. Portion evenly for accurate dosing.

recipes.name: Cannabis Beef Stew
recipes.description: Hearty cannabis-infused beef stew simmered with root vegetables and rich gravy, made with cannabutter for a warming and perfectly dosed edible meal.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1.5 lbs beef chuck, cut into 1-inch cubes
  • 3 medium potatoes, peeled and cubed
  • 3 carrots, peeled and sliced
  • 1 large onion, diced
  • 3 cups beef broth
  • 2 tablespoons tomato paste
  • 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 teaspoon dried thyme
recipes.instructions:
• Season beef cubes with salt and pepper. Dust lightly with flour.
  • Heat 1 tablespoon of regular oil in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown the beef on all sides in batches, about 3 minutes per batch. Remove and set aside.
  • Add onion and garlic to the pot, cooking for 3 minutes until softened. Stir in tomato paste and thyme.
  • Return beef to the pot. Pour in beef broth and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 1.5 hours.
  • Add potatoes and carrots. Continue simmering for 30-40 minutes until vegetables are tender.
  • Remove from heat and stir in the cannabutter until fully melted and incorporated into the stew.
  • Ladle into bowls, dividing evenly for consistent dosing. Serve with crusty bread.

recipes.name: Cannabis French Onion Soup
recipes.description: Classic cannabis-infused French onion soup with deeply caramelized onions, rich beef broth, and a bubbly Gruyère crust, made with cannabutter.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 4 large yellow onions, thinly sliced
  • 4 cups beef broth
  • 1/2 cup dry white wine
  • 2 tablespoons regular butter
  • 1 tablespoon fresh thyme leaves
  • 1 bay leaf
  • 4 slices French baguette, toasted
  • 1 cup Gruyère cheese, shredded
  • Salt and pepper to taste
recipes.instructions:
• Melt regular butter in a large pot over medium heat. Add sliced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, for 35-40 minutes until deeply caramelized and golden brown.
  • Add white wine and deglaze the pot, scraping up browned bits. Cook for 2 minutes until mostly evaporated.
  • Pour in beef broth, add thyme and bay leaf. Bring to a simmer and cook for 15 minutes. Remove bay leaf.
  • Remove from heat and stir in the cannabutter until fully melted and distributed throughout the soup.
  • Ladle soup into oven-safe bowls. Top each with a toasted baguette slice and shredded Gruyère.
  • Broil for 2-3 minutes until cheese is bubbly and golden. Serve immediately with caution as bowls will be very hot.

recipes.name: Cannabis Chicken Pot Pie
recipes.description: A comforting cannabis-infused chicken pot pie with a flaky golden crust and a creamy vegetable filling made with cannabutter for even dosing.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 2 cups cooked chicken, shredded or diced
  • 1 cup frozen peas and carrots
  • 1/2 cup celery, diced
  • 1/3 cup onion, diced
  • 1/3 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1.5 cups chicken broth
  • 2/3 cup whole milk
  • 2 refrigerated pie crusts
  • Salt and pepper to taste
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 375°F. Place one pie crust in the bottom of a 9-inch pie dish.
  • In a saucepan over medium heat, melt the cannabutter. Add onion and celery, cooking for 5 minutes until softened.
  • Stir in flour and cook for 1 minute. Gradually whisk in chicken broth and milk until smooth and thickened, about 5 minutes.
  • Add chicken, peas, carrots, salt, and pepper. Stir to combine and remove from heat.
  • Pour the filling into the pie crust. Top with the second crust, crimp edges, and cut several slits for steam to escape.
  • Bake for 35-40 minutes until the crust is golden brown. Let cool for 10 minutes before slicing into equal portions.

recipes.name: Cannabis Lasagna
recipes.description: Layered cannabis-infused lasagna with a rich meat sauce, creamy ricotta filling, and cannabutter for a classic Italian edible comfort meal.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 12 lasagna noodles
  • 1 lb ground beef
  • 2 cups marinara sauce
  • 15 oz ricotta cheese
  • 2 cups mozzarella cheese, shredded
  • 1/2 cup Parmesan cheese, grated
  • 1 egg
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • Fresh basil for garnish
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 375°F. Cook lasagna noodles according to package directions and drain.
  • Brown ground beef with garlic in a skillet over medium heat. Drain excess fat. Stir in marinara sauce and simmer for 10 minutes.
  • Melt the cannabutter and mix it into the ricotta cheese along with the egg, salt, and pepper until well combined.
  • In a greased 9x13 baking dish, layer: noodles, meat sauce, ricotta mixture, mozzarella. Repeat layers twice, ending with mozzarella and Parmesan on top.
  • Cover with foil and bake for 25 minutes. Remove foil and bake for another 15 minutes until cheese is bubbly and golden.
  • Let rest for 15 minutes before cutting into equal portions. Garnish with fresh basil.

recipes.name: Cannabis Carbonara
recipes.description: Authentic cannabis-infused carbonara with crispy pancetta, egg yolks, and Pecorino cheese, finished with cannabutter for a decadent edible pasta.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 12 oz spaghetti
  • 6 oz pancetta or guanciale, diced
  • 3 large egg yolks
  • 1 whole egg
  • 1 cup Pecorino Romano cheese, finely grated
  • Freshly cracked black pepper
  • Salt to taste
recipes.instructions:
• Cook spaghetti in well-salted water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
  • While pasta cooks, cook pancetta in a large skillet over medium heat until crispy, about 6-8 minutes. Remove from heat.
  • In a bowl, whisk together egg yolks, whole egg, and Pecorino cheese. Melt the cannabutter and whisk it into the egg mixture.
  • Add the hot drained pasta to the skillet with pancetta. Toss to coat in the rendered fat.
  • Remove skillet from heat. Pour the egg and cannabutter mixture over the pasta, tossing vigorously to create a creamy sauce. Add pasta water a tablespoon at a time if needed.
  • Serve immediately with extra Pecorino and freshly cracked black pepper. Divide into equal portions for consistent dosing.

recipes.name: Cannabis Chicken Tikka Masala
recipes.description: Aromatic cannabis-infused chicken tikka masala in a creamy tomato sauce with warm spices and cannabutter, served over basmati rice.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1.5 lbs boneless chicken thighs, cubed
  • 1 cup plain yogurt
  • 1 can (14 oz) crushed tomatoes
  • 1 cup heavy cream
  • 1 large onion, diced
  • 3 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 tablespoon garam masala
  • 1 teaspoon turmeric
  • 1 teaspoon cumin
recipes.instructions:
• Marinate chicken in yogurt, 1 teaspoon garam masala, turmeric, and salt for at least 30 minutes.
  • Broil or grill marinated chicken pieces for 5-6 minutes per side until charred and cooked through. Set aside.
  • In a large pan, melt cannabutter over medium heat. Sauté onion until softened, about 5 minutes. Add garlic, remaining garam masala, and cumin. Cook for 1 minute.
  • Add crushed tomatoes and simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
  • Stir in heavy cream and the cooked chicken. Simmer gently for 10 more minutes on low heat.
  • Serve over basmati rice, dividing evenly for consistent dosing. Garnish with fresh cilantro.

recipes.name: Cannabis Fish Tacos
recipes.description: Light and zesty cannabis-infused fish tacos with crispy battered cod, tangy slaw, and a cannabutter drizzle for a perfectly dosed edible lunch.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1 lb cod fillets, cut into strips
  • 8 small corn tortillas
  • 1 cup shredded cabbage
  • 1/2 cup sour cream
  • Juice of 2 limes
  • 1 teaspoon chili powder
  • 1/2 teaspoon cumin
  • 1 avocado, sliced
  • Fresh cilantro and hot sauce for topping
recipes.instructions:
• Season cod strips with chili powder, cumin, salt, and half the lime juice. Let rest for 10 minutes.
  • Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook cod strips for 3-4 minutes per side until flaky and cooked through.
  • Melt the cannabutter and mix with sour cream and remaining lime juice to create a cannabis-infused crema.
  • Toss shredded cabbage with a squeeze of lime and a pinch of salt for a quick slaw.
  • Warm tortillas in a dry skillet or directly over a gas flame.
  • Assemble tacos with fish, cabbage slaw, avocado slices, and drizzle with the cannabis crema. Garnish with cilantro and hot sauce.

recipes.name: Cannabis Teriyaki Bowl
recipes.description: A vibrant cannabis-infused teriyaki bowl with glazed chicken, steamed rice, and fresh vegetables, finished with cannabutter teriyaki sauce.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1.5 lbs boneless chicken thighs
  • 1/3 cup soy sauce
  • 3 tablespoons honey
  • 1 tablespoon rice vinegar
  • 1 teaspoon fresh ginger, grated
  • 2 cups cooked jasmine rice
  • 1 cup steamed broccoli florets
  • 1 carrot, julienned
  • Sesame seeds and green onions for garnish
recipes.instructions:
• Whisk together soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, and grated ginger to make the teriyaki sauce.
  • Cook chicken thighs in a skillet over medium-high heat for 6-7 minutes per side until cooked through. Slice into strips.
  • Pour teriyaki sauce into the skillet and bring to a simmer. Add cannabutter and stir until melted and combined with the sauce.
  • Return chicken strips to the skillet and toss to coat in the cannabis teriyaki glaze. Cook for 2 more minutes.
  • Divide rice among bowls. Top with glazed chicken, steamed broccoli, and julienned carrots.
  • Drizzle remaining sauce over each bowl. Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced green onions.

recipes.name: Cannabis Stuffed Bell Peppers
recipes.description: Colorful cannabis-infused stuffed bell peppers filled with seasoned beef, rice, and melted cheese, made with cannabutter for a hearty edible lunch.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 6 large bell peppers, tops removed and seeded
  • 1 lb ground beef
  • 1 cup cooked rice
  • 1 can (14 oz) diced tomatoes
  • 1 cup shredded cheddar cheese
  • 1 small onion, diced
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 teaspoon Italian seasoning
  • Salt and pepper to taste
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 375°F. Place bell peppers upright in a baking dish.
  • In a skillet, melt cannabutter over medium heat. Add onion and garlic, cooking for 3 minutes until softened.
  • Add ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it apart with a spoon. Drain excess fat.
  • Stir in cooked rice, diced tomatoes, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper. Cook for 5 minutes.
  • Stuff each bell pepper with the mixture and top with shredded cheddar cheese.
  • Cover with foil and bake for 30 minutes. Remove foil and bake for another 10 minutes until cheese is melted and peppers are tender.

recipes.name: Cannabis Clam Chowder
recipes.description: Thick and creamy cannabis-infused New England clam chowder with tender potatoes, smoky bacon, and cannabutter for a warming edible soup.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 2 cans (6.5 oz each) chopped clams, juice reserved
  • 4 slices bacon, diced
  • 2 medium potatoes, peeled and diced
  • 1 medium onion, diced
  • 2 stalks celery, diced
  • 2 cups whole milk
  • 1 cup heavy cream
  • 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
  • 1 bay leaf
recipes.instructions:
• Cook diced bacon in a large pot over medium heat until crispy. Remove bacon and set aside, leaving the drippings in the pot.
  • Add onion and celery to the bacon drippings and cook for 4 minutes until softened.
  • Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir for 1 minute. Gradually add reserved clam juice and milk, stirring until smooth.
  • Add potatoes and bay leaf. Simmer for 15-20 minutes until potatoes are tender.
  • Stir in clams, heavy cream, and cooked bacon. Heat through for 5 minutes without boiling.
  • Remove from heat and discard bay leaf. Stir in cannabutter until fully melted and incorporated.
  • Ladle into bowls, dividing evenly for accurate dosing. Serve with oyster crackers.

recipes.name: Cannabis Shepherd's Pie
recipes.description: Classic cannabis-infused shepherd's pie with savory ground lamb, mixed vegetables, and creamy mashed potatoes made with cannabutter.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1.5 lbs ground lamb
  • 4 large russet potatoes, peeled and cubed
  • 1 cup frozen peas and corn
  • 2 carrots, diced
  • 1 onion, diced
  • 2 tablespoons tomato paste
  • 1 cup beef broth
  • 1/2 cup whole milk
  • 2 tablespoons regular butter
recipes.instructions:
• Boil potatoes until tender, about 15 minutes. Drain and mash with milk, regular butter, cannabutter, salt, and pepper until smooth and creamy.
  • Preheat oven to 400°F. Brown ground lamb in a skillet over medium-high heat. Drain excess fat.
  • Add onion and carrots to the lamb, cooking for 5 minutes. Stir in tomato paste, beef broth, peas, and corn. Simmer for 10 minutes until thickened.
  • Transfer the lamb mixture to a baking dish. Spread the cannabis-infused mashed potatoes evenly over the top.
  • Use a fork to create texture on top of the mashed potatoes. Bake for 20-25 minutes until the top is golden and the filling is bubbling.
  • Let cool for 10 minutes before cutting into equal portions for consistent dosing.

recipes.name: Cannabis Keto Egg Muffins
recipes.description: Protein-packed cannabis-infused keto egg muffins loaded with cheese, bacon, and vegetables, made with cannabutter for a low-carb edible snack.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter, melted
  • 8 large eggs
  • 4 slices bacon, cooked and crumbled
  • 1/2 cup shredded cheddar cheese
  • 1/4 cup red bell pepper, finely diced
  • 2 tablespoons fresh chives, chopped
  • 2 tablespoons heavy cream
  • Salt and pepper to taste
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 12-cup muffin tin with cooking spray or line with silicone liners.
  • Whisk together eggs, heavy cream, melted cannabutter, salt, and pepper until well combined.
  • Divide crumbled bacon, diced bell pepper, and shredded cheese evenly among the muffin cups.
  • Pour the egg mixture evenly into each cup, filling about three-quarters full.
  • Bake for 18-22 minutes until eggs are set and tops are lightly golden.
  • Let cool in the tin for 5 minutes before removing. Sprinkle with fresh chives and serve.

recipes.name: Cannabis Keto Butter Chicken
recipes.description: Rich and creamy cannabis-infused keto butter chicken in a spiced tomato sauce, made with cannabutter for a low-carb, high-fat edible dinner.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 1.5 lbs boneless chicken thighs, cubed
  • 1/2 cup full-fat plain yogurt
  • 1 can (14 oz) crushed tomatoes
  • 1/2 cup heavy cream
  • 2 tablespoons regular butter
  • 1 tablespoon garam masala
  • 1 teaspoon turmeric
  • 3 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 tablespoon fresh ginger, grated
recipes.instructions:
• Marinate chicken in yogurt, garam masala, and turmeric for at least 20 minutes.
  • Heat regular butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook marinated chicken until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove and set aside.
  • In the same skillet, melt cannabutter over medium heat. Sauté garlic and ginger for 1 minute until fragrant.
  • Add crushed tomatoes and simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
  • Stir in heavy cream and return chicken to the skillet. Simmer on low for 15 minutes until chicken is cooked through and sauce has thickened.
  • Serve over cauliflower rice for a keto-friendly meal. Divide into equal portions for consistent dosing.

recipes.name: Cannabis Keto Avocado Brownies
recipes.description: Fudgy cannabis-infused keto avocado brownies made with almond flour, cocoa powder, and cannabutter for a guilt-free, low-carb edible treat.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter, melted
  • 2 ripe avocados, mashed
  • 1/2 cup unsweetened cocoa powder
  • 1/3 cup almond flour
  • 1/2 cup erythritol or monk fruit sweetener
  • 2 large eggs
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
  • Pinch of salt
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 325°F. Line an 8x8 baking pan with parchment paper.
  • In a food processor, blend mashed avocados until completely smooth.
  • Add melted cannabutter, eggs, and vanilla extract. Blend until combined.
  • In a separate bowl, whisk together cocoa powder, almond flour, sweetener, baking soda, and salt.
  • Fold the dry ingredients into the wet mixture until just combined. Do not overmix.
  • Pour batter into the prepared pan and bake for 20-25 minutes until a toothpick comes out with moist crumbs.
  • Let cool completely before cutting into equal squares for accurate dosing.

recipes.name: Cannabis Keto Bacon Cheeseburger Bites
recipes.description: Savory cannabis-infused keto bacon cheeseburger bites packed with ground beef, cheese, and crispy bacon, made with cannabutter for low-carb edible snacking.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter, melted
  • 1 lb ground beef
  • 6 slices bacon, cooked and crumbled
  • 1 cup shredded cheddar cheese
  • 1/4 cup dill pickles, finely diced
  • 2 tablespoons sugar-free ketchup
  • 1 tablespoon yellow mustard
  • 1 teaspoon garlic powder
  • 1 teaspoon onion powder
  • Salt and pepper to taste
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 375°F. Grease a 24-cup mini muffin tin.
  • In a large bowl, combine ground beef, melted cannabutter, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
  • Press the beef mixture into each mini muffin cup, forming small cups.
  • Bake for 12 minutes until beef is cooked through. Carefully drain any excess grease.
  • Top each bite with crumbled bacon, diced pickles, and shredded cheddar cheese. Return to oven for 3 minutes until cheese melts.
  • Drizzle with sugar-free ketchup and mustard. Let cool for 5 minutes before serving. Divide into equal portions for consistent dosing.

recipes.name: Cannabis Keto Garlic Parmesan Wings
recipes.description: Crispy cannabis-infused keto garlic Parmesan wings baked until golden and tossed in a savory cannabutter garlic sauce for a low-carb edible appetizer.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabutter
  • 2 lbs chicken wings
  • 1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese
  • 4 cloves garlic, minced
  • 2 tablespoons regular butter
  • 1 tablespoon olive oil
  • 1 teaspoon Italian seasoning
  • 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder
  • Salt and pepper to taste
  • Fresh parsley for garnish
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 425°F. Pat chicken wings dry and toss with olive oil, garlic powder, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper.
  • Arrange wings in a single layer on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Bake for 40-45 minutes, flipping halfway, until crispy and golden.
  • While wings bake, melt cannabutter and regular butter together in a small saucepan over low heat. Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
  • Remove garlic butter from heat and stir in grated Parmesan cheese.
  • Toss the baked wings in the cannabis garlic Parmesan butter until evenly coated.
  • Garnish with fresh parsley and serve immediately. Divide wings equally for consistent dosing.

recipes.name: Cannabis Vegan Pad Thai
recipes.description: A vibrant cannabis-infused vegan pad Thai with rice noodles, crispy tofu, and a tangy tamarind sauce made with cannabis-infused coconut oil.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabis-infused coconut oil
  • 8 oz flat rice noodles
  • 14 oz extra-firm tofu, pressed and cubed
  • 2 tablespoons tamarind paste
  • 2 tablespoons soy sauce
  • 1 tablespoon maple syrup
  • 1 cup bean sprouts
  • 2 green onions, sliced
  • 1/4 cup roasted peanuts, crushed
  • 1 lime, cut into wedges
recipes.instructions:
• Soak rice noodles in warm water for 20 minutes until softened. Drain and set aside.
  • Heat 1 tablespoon regular coconut oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Fry tofu cubes until golden on all sides, about 5-6 minutes. Remove and set aside.
  • Whisk together tamarind paste, soy sauce, maple syrup, and cannabis-infused coconut oil until smooth.
  • In the same wok, add the drained noodles and the tamarind-cannabis sauce. Toss over medium heat for 2-3 minutes until noodles are coated and heated through.
  • Add tofu and bean sprouts, tossing to combine. Cook for 1 more minute.
  • Serve topped with crushed peanuts, sliced green onions, and lime wedges. Divide into equal portions for consistent dosing.

recipes.name: Cannabis Vegan Buddha Bowl
recipes.description: A nourishing cannabis-infused vegan Buddha bowl with roasted chickpeas, quinoa, and seasonal vegetables, drizzled with cannabis coconut oil tahini dressing.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabis-infused coconut oil
  • 1 can (15 oz) chickpeas, drained and rinsed
  • 1 cup quinoa, cooked
  • 1 sweet potato, cubed and roasted
  • 1 cup kale, massaged and chopped
  • 1 avocado, sliced
  • 2 tablespoons tahini
  • 1 tablespoon lemon juice
  • 1 teaspoon smoked paprika
  • Salt and pepper to taste
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 400°F. Toss chickpeas with smoked paprika, salt, and 1 tablespoon regular oil. Spread on a baking sheet and roast for 25 minutes until crispy.
  • Toss sweet potato cubes with a drizzle of oil, salt, and pepper. Roast on a separate baking sheet for 20-25 minutes until tender.
  • Gently warm the cannabis-infused coconut oil and whisk it together with tahini, lemon juice, 1 tablespoon water, and a pinch of salt to make the dressing.
  • Divide quinoa among bowls. Arrange roasted sweet potato, crispy chickpeas, massaged kale, and avocado slices on top.
  • Drizzle each bowl with equal amounts of the cannabis tahini dressing.
  • Serve immediately. Each bowl should receive an equal portion of dressing for consistent dosing.

recipes.name: Cannabis Vegan Stuffed Peppers
recipes.description: Colorful cannabis-infused vegan stuffed peppers filled with seasoned rice, black beans, and corn, made with cannabis-infused coconut oil.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabis-infused coconut oil
  • 6 large bell peppers, tops removed and seeded
  • 1 cup cooked brown rice
  • 1 can (15 oz) black beans, drained and rinsed
  • 1 cup corn kernels
  • 1 can (14 oz) diced tomatoes
  • 1 teaspoon cumin
  • 1 teaspoon chili powder
  • 1/4 cup nutritional yeast
  • Fresh cilantro for garnish
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 375°F. Place bell peppers upright in a baking dish.
  • Heat cannabis-infused coconut oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add cumin and chili powder, stirring for 30 seconds until fragrant.
  • Add cooked rice, black beans, corn, and diced tomatoes to the skillet. Stir to combine and cook for 5 minutes.
  • Stuff each bell pepper with the rice and bean mixture. Sprinkle nutritional yeast on top.
  • Cover the baking dish with foil and bake for 30 minutes. Remove foil and bake for 10 more minutes until peppers are tender.
  • Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve. Each pepper is one portion for easy dosing.

recipes.name: Cannabis Vegan Oat Cookies
recipes.description: Chewy cannabis-infused vegan oat cookies with raisins and cinnamon, made with cannabis-infused coconut oil for a wholesome plant-based edible treat.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabis-infused coconut oil, melted
  • 1.5 cups rolled oats
  • 3/4 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1/3 cup maple syrup
  • 1/4 cup coconut sugar
  • 1/4 cup raisins
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 1 teaspoon cinnamon
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
  • Pinch of salt
recipes.instructions:
• Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
  • In a large bowl, mix together rolled oats, flour, cinnamon, baking soda, and salt.
  • In a separate bowl, whisk together melted cannabis-infused coconut oil, maple syrup, coconut sugar, and vanilla extract.
  • Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined. Fold in raisins.
  • Scoop tablespoon-sized balls onto the baking sheet, spacing them 2 inches apart. Flatten slightly with the back of a spoon.
  • Bake for 12-14 minutes until edges are golden. Let cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack.
  • Makes approximately 16 cookies. Divide batch evenly for consistent dosing.

recipes.name: Cannabis Vegan Mushroom Stroganoff
recipes.description: Creamy cannabis-infused vegan mushroom stroganoff with a rich cashew cream sauce and cannabis-infused coconut oil, served over egg-free pasta.
recipes.ingredients:
• 3.5 grams of cannabis-infused coconut oil
  • 16 oz mixed mushrooms (cremini, shiitake), sliced
  • 12 oz egg-free pasta
  • 1 cup raw cashews, soaked for 2 hours
  • 1 cup vegetable broth
  • 1 large onion, diced
  • 3 cloves garlic, minced
  • 2 tablespoons soy sauce
  • 1 tablespoon Dijon mustard
  • Fresh thyme and parsley for garnish
recipes.instructions:
• Drain soaked cashews and blend with vegetable broth until completely smooth to create cashew cream. Set aside.
  • Cook pasta according to package directions. Drain and set aside.
  • Heat cannabis-infused coconut oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Sauté onion for 4 minutes until translucent, then add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
  • Add sliced mushrooms and cook for 6-8 minutes until golden and most liquid has evaporated.
  • Pour in the cashew cream, soy sauce, and Dijon mustard. Stir and simmer for 5 minutes until the sauce thickens.
  • Toss the cooked pasta into the stroganoff sauce. Serve garnished with fresh thyme and parsley, dividing evenly for consistent dosing.
